issue.body:
Tracks: #35

Design the `__repr__` contract for `BoundingBox` and related geometry types.

**Open design questions:**
- Should `__repr__` be self-evaluating (i.e., `eval(repr(x)) == x`)?
- Should all geometry types (`Rect`, `Point`, etc.) get consistent `__repr__` treatment?
- What format: `BoundingBox(0, 0, 10, 10)` or `BoundingBox(x0=0, y0=0, x1=10, y1=10)`?

**Scope:**
- `BoundingBox.__repr__` implementation in `pd_book_tools/geometry/bounding_box.py`
- Unit test confirming the format
- Decision on whether sibling geometry types need parallel treatment

Spec: docs/specs/08-geometry-repr.md

Spec: docs/specs/08-geometry-repr.md

issue.title: Spec: BoundingBox (and sibling geometry types) __repr__ contract

issue.body:
## Feature

`BoundingBox` instances appear in pytest assertion failures as
`<BoundingBox object at 0x...>`. A simple `__repr__` returning the
`(x0, y0, x1, y1)` tuple would make assertion diffs immediately readable
without dropping into the debugger.

## Motivation

Reduces debug cycle time for layout-regression failures (where BoundingBox
mismatches are the most common failure mode).

## Acceptance

- `repr(BoundingBox(0, 0, 10, 10))` returns `'BoundingBox(0, 0, 10, 10)'`
- One unit test asserting the format.

(Test issue for R2 smoke validation of /triage → /spec-from-issue → /decompose-spec chain.)

Spec: docs/specs/08-geometry-repr.md

issue.title: Geometry Types __repr__: Add unit tests for BoundingBox and Point repr

issue.body:
Tracks: #36
Spec: docs/specs/08-geometry-repr.md

## Summary

Spec #36 (merged in PR #37 as commit `c3b0e8f7`) specifies that `BoundingBox.__repr__` returns:

```text
BoundingBox.from_ltrb(x0, y0, x1, y1)
```

so that `eval(repr(bb)) == bb` holds.

The implementation shipped in #38 (commit `764f8c3` on `wip/ship-issue`, PR #50) returns the bare form `BoundingBox(x0, y0, x1, y1)` per the earlier (pre-merge) spec version. That form is misleading — primary constructor doesn't accept four scalars, so `eval()` fails with TypeError.

## Repro

```python
from pd_book_tools.geometry import BoundingBox, Point
bb = BoundingBox(Point(0, 0), Point(10, 10))
print(repr(bb))   # current: 'BoundingBox(0, 0, 10, 10)'  (wrong)
                  # spec:    'BoundingBox.from_ltrb(0, 0, 10, 10)'
eval(repr(bb))    # current: TypeError (wrong)
                  # spec: returns equivalent BoundingBox
```

## Fix

Update `pd_book_tools/geometry/bounding_box.py`'s `__repr__` to return:

```python
def __repr__(self):
    return f"BoundingBox.from_ltrb({self.minX}, {self.minY}, {self.maxX}, {self.maxY})"
```

Update or add the unit test in `tests/test_geometry_repr.py` to assert the new form. Verify `eval(repr(bb)) == bb` for a basic case.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `repr(BoundingBox(Point(0, 0), Point(10, 10))) == 'BoundingBox.from_ltrb(0, 0, 10, 10)'`
- [ ] `eval(repr(bb)) == bb` for any `bb` constructed without `is_normalized`
- [ ] Existing tests still pass

## Surfaced by

Spec PR #37 review — caught format mismatch before #40's unit tests locked the wrong form.

labels.description: Bot-eligibility gate — ship-issue may modify this issue
labels.name: bot:ship-issue-ready
